puzzle.title: The Echo from the Edge of the City
puzzle.intro: In a city that turned away from divine guidance, a solitary figure emerged from the outskirts, racing with a message of hope and warning. This forgotten soul would become an eternal symbol of steadfast faith.
clues: I witnessed messengers being sent to my city, but my people dismissed them as ordinary humans.
I believed in their message and felt the truth resonate within my being.
I called upon my people to obey the messengers who asked for no reward.
I ran from the farthest end of the city, urgently trying to make them see reason.
My people rose up against me, and I was slain, but I found eternal peace.
Allah said to me, 'Enter Paradise,' and I wished my people could witness the honor bestowed upon me.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Confronting a disbelieving people who slew him
Being thrown into a blazing fire
Being swallowed by a great fish
trial.answer: Confronting a disbelieving people who slew him

location.label: Key Location
options: The farthest end of the city
location.answer: The farthest end of the city

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Martyrdom and immediate entry into Paradise
outcome.answer: Martyrdom and immediate entry into Paradise

puzzle.arabic: قِيلَ ٱدْخُلِ ٱلْجَنَّةَ ۖ قَالَ يَـٰلَيْتَ قَوْمِى يَعْلَمُونَ
puzzle.verse: It was said, "Enter Paradise." He said, "I wish my people could know (36:26)
